> **Estimated time:** 4-8 h · **Difficulty:** 🟢 mainstream · **Labels:** `good-first-issue` `help wanted` `provider`
>
> **Maintainer pair-review available** — comment below before you start; you will not be left alone with the first PR.

## What we want

A working **cPanel adapter skeleton** living at `providers/cpanel/` that implements the [`providers.HostingProvider`](https://github.com/dilitS/webox/blob/main/providers/provider.go) interface against the [**cPanel UAPI**](https://api.docs.cpanel.net/cpanel/introduction/). After this issue is closed:

- `go build ./...` continues to succeed.
- `webox doctor` reports cPanel as a *registered* provider.
- One read-only method (`CheckStatus`) actually calls UAPI end-to-end with sanitised fixtures.
- The other interface methods return `cpanel.ErrNotImplemented` (the sentinel the generator scaffolds — see below).

Real production-grade implementations of `CreateSubdomain`, `SetupSSL`, etc. land in **Sprint 17-18** (see [`docs/ROADMAP.md`](https://github.com/dilitS/webox/blob/main/docs/ROADMAP.md)). This issue is the *runway* — get the package on disk, the build green, and `CheckStatus` proven against a real UAPI fixture so the maintainer can review the contract before the deeper PRs land.

## Why cPanel first

cPanel powers ~70 % of shared hosting in North America and EMEA. WHM is the de-facto reseller standard. Shipping a cPanel adapter unlocks roughly 30 M websites in a single PR — by far the biggest leverage move for the v0.2 release.

## How to start (30 seconds)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/dilitS/webox.git
cd webox
make build
./bin/webox provider new cpanel --preset cpanel-uapi
```

That command:

1. Scaffolds `providers/cpanel/{provider,parsers,doc,fixture}.go` with UAPI-shaped stubs.
2. Patches the blank-import block in `cmd/webox/providers.go` (sorted, idempotent).
3. Prints a next-step walkthrough.

After that, follow the **4-hour walkthrough** at [`docs/contributing/PROVIDER.md`](https://github.com/dilitS/webox/blob/main/docs/contributing/PROVIDER.md). Sections especially relevant here:

- §2 *Preset vs. raw adapter* — `cpanel-uapi` preset already chose your stack.
- §3 *Fixture sourcing & sanitisation* — paid cPanel sandbox is fine; sanitise account names with `scripts/sanitize-fixture.sh` before committing.
- §5 *`sshmock` integration* — UAPI is HTTP, not SSH, so you use `services/httpmock` instead.
- §7 *Capability probe* — `CheckStatus` is the single mandatory probe method.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `providers/cpanel/` exists, generated by `webox provider new cpanel --preset cpanel-uapi`.
- [ ] `go build ./...` passes.
- [ ] `cpanel.Provider.CheckStatus(ctx)` actually calls UAPI (`Notifications::get_notifications_count` or any cheap read-only endpoint) using `services/httpmock` in tests and a real fixture in `providers/cpanel/testdata/`.
- [ ] At least one fixture captured from a real cPanel sandbox (any free trial works) and **sanitised** — no real usernames, domains, or tokens.
- [ ] All other interface methods return a sentinel `cpanel.ErrNotImplemented` wrapped with `fmt.Errorf("cpanel.%s: %w", method, ErrNotImplemented)`.
- [ ] Tests use table-driven style and cover at least the parser for `CheckStatus` response (happy path + one error path).
- [ ] Coverage for the new package ≥ 70 %.
- [ ] `make lint && make test` green locally.
- [ ] CHANGELOG `[Unreleased] / Added` entry following the [`auto-changelog`](https://github.com/dilitS/webox/blob/main/.cursor/skills/auto-changelog/SKILL.md) skill.
- [ ] `docs/providers/cpanel.md` updated — replace the `TO BE VERIFIED` markers under §1 *Capability probe* and §2 *Read-only authentication*.

## What we will NOT accept

- Hardcoded credentials in tests or fixtures (use `httpmock` + sanitised JSON).
- `Update()` / `View()` modifications — this work stays in `providers/` and `services/`.
- Auto-accept on cert validation errors against the cPanel host.
- Real account names or domains in fixtures.
